domain: Research domains: Cell Biology | Developmental Biology Objective: 为什么只有部分细胞能够分化、转分化或被重编程为其他细胞类型，而多数已分化细胞的命运受到限制？请检验转录因子调控网络、染色质可及性、表观遗传记忆、DNA methylation、细胞谱系历史、干细胞生态位信号、细胞外基质、代谢状态和炎症微环境如何共同决定细胞可塑性，并解释胚胎干细胞、成人干细胞和诱导多能干细胞之间潜能差异。

research_question_card.research_question: iPSC重编程屏障与pioneer factors的作用 pioneer factors的引入或过表达 -> 靶向沉默区域的开放 -> 提高诱导多能干细胞生成效率 即使在pioneer factors过表达的情况下，诱导多能干细胞生成效率也没有显著提高
research_question_card.domain: Research domains: Cell Biology | Developmental Biology Objective: 为什么只有部分细胞能够分化、转分化或被重编程为其他细胞类型，而多数已分化细胞的命运受到限制？请检验转录因子调控网络、染色质可及性、表观遗传记忆、DNA methylation、细胞谱系历史、干细胞生态位信号、细胞外基质、代谢状态和炎症微环境如何共同决定细胞可塑性，并解释胚胎干细胞、成人干细胞和诱导多能干细胞之间潜能差异。
causal_questions: What is necessary or sufficient for the proposed effect?
What mediates the relationship, and what alternative paths remain plausible?
Under which model, stage, time, or boundary condition does the relationship fail?

boundary_policy.instruction: Use the stated project boundary when assigning a paper role.

paper_role_policy.CORE: Directly supports the bounded research question or supplies context-matched mechanism evidence.
paper_role_policy.BRIDGE: Supplies transferable mechanism, terminology, or background but cannot alone support the core causal claim.
paper_role_policy.EXCLUDE: Conflicts with an explicit project boundary or final domain gate and must not enter core reasoning.

year_policy.purpose: upper-bound guard; recency rules provide the lower bound

results.title: CPTCs Drive Somatic-Visceral Communication via the Wnt Axis in Somatic Mechanotherapy: A Single-Cell Deep Learning Study
results.citation: Haixiang Huang et al. (2026) CPTCs Drive Somatic-Visceral Communication via the Wnt Axis in Somatic Mechanotherapy: A Single-Cell Deep Learning Study arXiv:2602.05451v2
results.abstract: Somatic mechanical stimulation (e.g., acupuncture) exerts systemic immunomodulatory effects, yet the cellular bridge translating peripheral physical force into visceral repair remains elusive. Here, employing a custom interpretable deep learning framework (CARSS) on single-cell RNA sequencing data, we identify CD34$^{+}$PDGFR$α$$^{+}$ telocytes (CPTCs) as the primary mechanosensors in both fascia and colon during bacterial colitis. We show that somatic mechanotherapy triggers an AP-1/Hsp70-dependent transcriptional program in fascial CPTCs, inducing systemic Wnt elevation, which elicits a "transcriptional resonance" in colonic CPTCs, reprogramming their communication network from an inflammatory amplifier to a Wnt-driven regenerative hub. Mechanistically, this axis activates epithelial $β$-catenin/Myc signaling, suppressing apoptosis and restoring barrier integrity independent of immune cells. Our findings define a CPTC-Driven Mechano-Resonance Axis, where CPTCs serve as synchronized relay stations that convert local mechanical cues into systemic regenerative microenvironments.
